_intent: Shared verification harness for Momentum-managed projects. The 'defaults' block is the all-surfaces-skip posture — a deliberate carve-out for markdown/bash-only repos (like this one) that have no application to run. Skipping every surface is CORRECT for such repos and is not a silent verification gap. A real product repo (one with an app, API, or scripts to execute) MUST add an entry to the 'projects' array to activate real runners for the surfaces that apply. Leaving every surface skipped in a product repo is a silent gap — the 'projects' array is where that is fixed.

defaults._note: All-surfaces-skip is the intentional carve-out for a project with no executable surface (e.g. a markdown-only or bash-utility repo). A product repo must override via the 'projects' array below.

skill-invoke.description: Invoke a skill directly in the agent context

behavioral-trigger.description: Trigger and observe rule/hook behavior via cmux terminal surface

bash.description: Execute scripts/CLI via cmux terminal surface

smoke.description: UI smoke test via Maestro (mobile/web); falls back to Playwright for web-only targets

document-review.description: Human document review — no automated driver

platform_matrix.notes: Project verification-harness.json may override to declare specific platforms (android, ios, web, desktop)

trivial_smoke_escape.notes: Set enabled=true and list change_types to bypass smoke validation for trivial stories of those types

projects._template_note: The block below is a TEMPLATE showing how a real product project configures this harness. Copy it, replace 'example-product-app' with your project slug, and fill in the env values for your stack. Remove this _template_note key before activating.
projects.description: Example: a product repo with a mobile/web UI, a REST backend, and shell scripts — all three runnable surface types configured with real drivers.

env._note: Startup commands and readiness probes apply to surfaces that require a running environment (e.g. app-ui needs the frontend, backend needs the API server). Pure script surfaces (script-code, script-cli) run via cmux bash and do not require service readiness — leave their probes absent. The 'npm run dev &' backgrounding below is intentional; readiness for HTTP services is enforced solely by the curl --retry probes.

execution_surfaces._note: Project entries may use either string values (matching a driver_bindings key — e.g. 'bash', 'curl', 'smoke') or object values for surfaces that need driver-specific options (driver, fallback, scenario_path, base_url_env, platform). The e2e-validator reads the 'driver' key from an object entry the same way it reads a string entry — both resolve to a driver_bindings entry. Surfaces not listed here inherit the shared default (skip). Setting a surface to 'skip' is valid only if the project has no stories of that change_type.

app-ui.description: UI smoke tests via Maestro for mobile targets; Playwright for web-only flows. Maps to the 'smoke' driver_binding.

backend.description: HTTP endpoint verification via curl against the running backend. Maps to the 'curl' driver_binding.

script-cli.description: Run CLI scripts in a cmux terminal surface and assert exit code + stdout pattern. Maps to the 'bash' driver_binding.

script-code.description: Execute and observe script-code artifacts (e.g. migration scripts, data transforms) via cmux bash surface. Maps to the 'bash' driver_binding.
